categories.summary: This Discord chat segment contains minimal substantive technical discussion. The conversation consists primarily of introductory messages and casual greetings. User rsn6958 posted a self-introduction describing their background in AI and full-stack development, emphasizing practical problem-solving, clean code practices, and production-ready systems. The message highlights focus areas including efficiency, accuracy, user experience, maintainability, and security. User brickolascage made a brief comment about someone's computer skills, though the context is unclear. User dondada0420 greeted the channel and inquired about chatters. No technical problems were solved, no architectural decisions were made, and no concrete implementations were discussed. The segment lacks meaningful technical discourse, code reviews, debugging sessions, or collaborative problem-solving that would typically characterize active development discussions.

The main technical discussion centered on v2 registry infrastructure for third-party Eliza plugins. thegreatluna8713 reported that plugin-undesirables@2.0.3 is ready for submission to the v2 registry but encountered 404 errors when attempting to access the elizaos-plugins/registry repo and plugins.elizacloud.ai. They have generated JSON metadata prepared and noted their plugin uses BUSL-1.1 licensing, preventing direct PR to the monorepo. odilitime acknowledged the issue and indicated they would investigate, suggesting it might be accidental. They also mentioned a potential policy change to revert back to PRs directly to elizaOS/eliza repository. The conversation was interrupted by security concerns when shrektwo noticed suspicious activity, identifying compromised admin accounts. odilitime confirmed ongoing scammer issues in the Discord. The technical resolution for the registry submission process remains pending investigation.

faqs.question: I've got a third-party plugin (plugin-undesirables@2.0.3) ready for the v2 registry. The target elizaos-plugins/registry repo and plugins.elizacloud.ai are returning 404s. Is the v2 registry infra still private pending the 2.0.0-beta launch? Should I hold my PR until the repo goes public?

helpInteractions.context: Unable to access v2 registry infrastructure (404 errors on elizaos-plugins/registry and plugins.elizacloud.ai) for third-party plugin submission
helpInteractions.resolution: Acknowledged the issue and committed to investigating; suggested potential policy change to revert to PRs to elizaOS/eliza

actionItems.description: Investigate 404 errors on elizaos-plugins/registry repo and plugins.elizacloud.ai for v2 registry access
Determine final submission process for v2 third-party plugins and communicate to plugin developers
Address Discord security issues with compromised admin accounts

categories.summary: The discussion centered on Eliza v3 development progress and the new Eliza-1 model series. mahmoudamer7645 raised critical questions about v3's current focus, specifically whether development prioritizes infrastructure stabilization or is approaching public release of the full autonomous agent workflow stack. They also inquired about the integration status of the new identity/AgentID system in the upcoming architecture. zadayos announced the latest Eliza-1 model series based on Qwen3.5, detailing a comprehensive range from 0.6B (mobile-optimized) to 27B-1M (million token context). The series includes a new 0.8B model currently training on H200 hardware, with models scaled for different deployment scenarios from phones to servers. The technical specifications indicate a strategic approach to model deployment across various hardware constraints, with particular emphasis on offline capability for the smallest models and extended context windows for the largest variants.

faqs.question: Can you clarify whether the current v3 progress is mainly focused on infrastructure stabilization first, or are we close to seeing the full autonomous agent workflow stack exposed publicly?
How much of the new identity / AgentID system is already integrated into the upcoming architecture?

actionItems.description: Complete v3 infrastructure stabilization and autonomous agent workflow stack development
Integrate identity/AgentID system into v3 architecture
Complete training of Eliza-1 0.8B model on H200 hardware

categories.summary: blankey1717 introduced GODL, a gamified on-chain mining protocol on Solana, proposing integration with elizaOS. GODL exposes system functionality through SDKs, websocket streams, automation hooks, and a skill.md file specifically designed for agent/tool integrations. The protocol enables autonomous agents to interact with mining/staking mechanics, including autonomous mining/staking strategies, agent-managed wallets, competing mining behaviors, and public leaderboards/performance tracking. odilitime confirmed that elizaOS can use the skill.md format directly as-is, indicating compatibility with their existing agent architecture. blankey1717 emphasized that GODL's skill.md + SDK/websocket setup naturally maps to elizaOS's agent/tool usage framework, suggesting potential for experiments with autonomous mining/staking behaviors and competing agent strategies. The conversation concluded with blankey1717 offering to connect the elizaOS team with GODL developers and moving to DMs to share links and agent information without bot interference.

faqs.question: Can elizaOS use GODL's skill.md format?

helpInteractions.context: Providing information about GODL protocol integration possibilities with elizaOS
helpInteractions.resolution: Shared details about GODL's SDK, websocket streams, and skill.md setup for agent integrations, offered to connect with GODL developers

actionItems.description: Explore integration between elizaOS and GODL protocol using skill.md format
Review GODL SDK/websocket setup for autonomous agent mining/staking strategies
Share GODL docs and skill.md with elizaOS team

categories.summary: The channel featured a significant technical post about a novel escrow protocol designed specifically for AI agents deployed on Base Mainnet. The protocol implements a 'Pay or Burn' mechanism that eliminates human arbitration bottlenecks by using game theory - if agents cannot agree on task outcomes, escrowed funds are burned, destroying the expected value for malicious actors. Key architectural decisions include 100% pull-payment design for protection against reverting receiver contracts and zero UI approach with only raw smart contracts and SDKs in Python/C++. The creator is seeking architectural feedback from the community. A separate technical discussion involved Neynar API credit consumption issues, where a user reported unexpectedly high usage (300 credits/hour vs expected 4/hour) on v2/farcaster/notifications endpoint despite overriding the default 120-second polling interval. A community member suggested webhooks as a potential solution to reduce credit consumption. The channel also saw job postings for blockchain gaming positions and AI/ML engineering services.

faqs.question: Is there any documentation on Neynar credits consumption? My agent is consuming around 300 credits per hour on v2/farcaster/notifications, but should only be doing 4 per hour even after overriding the default 120 second call.
faqs.answeredBy: odilitime suggested webhooks could reduce consumption

helpInteractions.context: High Neynar API credit consumption (300/hour instead of expected 4/hour) on notifications endpoint
helpInteractions.resolution: Suggested using webhooks as an alternative to reduce credit usage

actionItems.description: Review and provide architectural critiques for the AI agent escrow protocol with Pay or Burn mechanism on Base Mainnet
Provide clearer documentation on Neynar credits consumption and billing for API endpoints
Investigate webhook implementation to reduce Neynar API credit consumption

categories.summary: The discussion centered on two main topics: a partnership announcement and V3 release timing. ivgamer3467 provided details about a deal between Secure Blockchain Development Corp. and the Eliza Foundation to commercialize an Agentic SME solution using the ElizaOS framework. The partnership timeline includes a 30-day period for v1 development followed by 90 days for full deployment. This represents a significant commercial application of the ElizaOS framework for small and medium enterprises. The discussion also touched on V3 release expectations, with lalanotia noting it had been 2 weeks since Shaw indicated an imminent release. There was some skepticism from ame05230523 regarding the partnership details, but ivgamer3467 clarified it involves a Canadian listed entity with outlined timelines. A minor moderation inquiry about unbanning a user named 'arisae' was addressed by odilitime, who confirmed no user by that name was found in the ban list.

faqs.question: When will the V3 be released?
Is anyone with the name arisae banned?

helpInteractions.context: Request to unban user named arisae
helpInteractions.resolution: Checked ban list and confirmed no user with that name was found banned

actionItems.description: Complete v1 of Agentic SME solution within 30-day timeline for Secure Blockchain Development Corp partnership
Full deployment of Agentic SME solution using ElizaOS framework within 90 days following v1
